title: Alphabet lifts capex guide to $205bn as Google Cloud jumps 82%
excerpt: Alphabet went into its second-quarter results on Wednesday carrying one question louder than the rest: whether the tens of billions it is funnelling into AI infrastructure has started to earn its keep. Judged by the after-hours share price, the answer was not yet. The company reported revenue of $119.8bn for the three months to June, up 24% […]
content_html:
Alphabet went into its second-quarter results on Wednesday carrying one question louder than the rest: whether the tens of billions it is funnelling into AI infrastructure has started to earn its keep. Judged by the after-hours share price, the answer was not yet.

The company reported revenue of $119.8bn for the three months to June, up 24% from $96.4bn a year earlier and comfortably ahead of forecasts.

Google Cloud did the heavy lifting, with revenue climbing 82% to $24.8bn, operating income more than tripling to $8.8bn, and its margin widening to about 36%. Group operating margin edged up to 34% from 32%.

That extends a streak that had Alphabet closing in on Nvidia as the world’s most valuable company, and it slots into a Big Tech capex cycle now running past $650bn a year. Cloud backlog, the contracted work Google has yet to book, rose to $514bn from $490bn.

Going in, the pressure was explicit. Bloomberg framed the quarter as a test of whether the spending pays off, and Alphabet was hardly alone in facing it, with investors weighing the same question at Tesla and across the rest of the Magnificent Seven the same week.

The 💜 of EU tech

The latest rumblings from the EU tech scene, a story from our wise ol' founder Boris, and some questionable AI art. It's free, every week, in your inbox. Sign up now!

The spending, not the growth, is what rattled them. Alphabet lifted its full-year capital-expenditure guidance to as much as $205bn, up from a prior range of $180bn to $190bn, and said quarterly capex had roughly doubled from a year earlier to $44.9bn.

Even $205bn does not cover it. Alphabet said it would keep expanding rented, third-party capacity as a bridge while its own data centres come online, a measure of how quickly demand is outrunning what it can build.

The bill pushed free cash flow to negative $5.9bn, the first quarterly outflow in nearly two decades.

Shares fell about 5% in extended trading despite the revenue beat, part of a now-familiar rhythm this earnings season of clean beats undone by capex lines that land heavier than expected.

The scale is the story. Alphabet is on course to spend more on capital investment in a single year than it books in net income over a comparable stretch, funding the build largely from a search and advertising business growing far more slowly. Analysts have started asking when, exactly, that gap closes.

The backlog is the counterargument the bulls reach for. A book of $514bn in contracted, not-yet-recognised revenue suggests the capacity being built already has buyers waiting; the bearish read is that it is a promise Alphabet still has to fund and deliver while the meter runs.

What both sides agree on is that the answer hinges on cloud becoming self-sustaining before the capex wave crests.

The headline profit figure did not settle the argument. Net income came in at $112.1bn, close to quadruple a year earlier, but roughly $98bn of that was an unrealised paper gain on Alphabet’s stake in SpaceX. Strip it out and the underlying number looks a good deal more ordinary.

The core ad engine held. Search revenue rose 17% to $63.3bn and YouTube advertising 13% to $11.1bn, while the Gemini app reached 950 million monthly active users and Alphabet’s first-party model APIs processed some 22 billion tokens a minute.

Sundar Pichai said AI features in Search were driving incremental queries while still sending billions of clicks to websites each week, and that AI Mode had passed 1 billion monthly users.

To pay for the build, Alphabet has already leaned on a record $85bn equity raise and a debut yen bond. CFO Anat Ashkenazi told investors no further equity offerings were planned beyond a $40bn at-the-market programme starting this quarter.

For all the noise, the results left open the one question they were meant to answer. The AI spending is clearly producing growth.

When it begins paying for itself, and how much more Alphabet is willing to spend while it waits to find out, is still unresolved.

blocks.html_en: To pay for the build, Alphabet has already leaned on a record $85bn equity raise and a debut yen bond. CFO Anat Ashkenazi told investors no further equity offerings were planned beyond a $40bn at-the-market programme starting this quarter.

sentences.en: To pay for the build, Alphabet has already leaned on a record $85bn equity raise and a debut yen bond.
sentences.zh: 为了支付建设费用，Alphabet 已经依靠创纪录的 850 亿美元融资和首次发行日元债券。

sentences.en: CFO Anat Ashkenazi told investors no further equity offerings were planned beyond a $40bn at-the-market programme starting this quarter.
sentences.zh: 首席财务官阿纳特·阿什肯纳齐 (Anat Ashkenazi) 告诉投资者，除了从本季度开始的 400 亿美元市场计划之外，没有计划进一步发行股票。

blocks.html_en: For all the noise, the results left open the one question they were meant to answer. The AI spending is clearly producing growth.

sentences.en: For all the noise, the results left open the one question they were meant to answer.
sentences.zh: 尽管有这么多的噪音，结果却留下了他们本应回答的一个问题。

sentences.en: The AI spending is clearly producing growth.
sentences.zh: 人工智能支出显然正在带来增长。

blocks.html_en: When it begins paying for itself, and how much more Alphabet is willing to spend while it waits to find out, is still unresolved.

sentences.en: When it begins paying for itself, and how much more Alphabet is willing to spend while it waits to find out, is still unresolved.
sentences.zh: 当它开始收回成本时，以及在等待答案的过程中，Alphabet 愿意再花多少钱，仍然是一个悬而未决的问题。

content_html_zh:
Alphabet 周三公布第二季度业绩时，提出了一个比其他公司更响亮的问题：它投入人工智能基础设施的数百亿美元是否已经开始盈利。 从盘后股价来看，目前还没有答案。

该公司公布截至 6 月份的三个月营收为 1,198 亿美元，较上年同期的 964 亿美元增长 24%，大大超出预期。

谷歌云承担了重任，收入增长 82%，达到 248 亿美元，运营收入增长两倍多，达到 88 亿美元，利润率扩大到约 36%。 集团营业利润率从 32% 微升至 34%。

这延续了 Alphabet 逼近英伟达成为全球最有价值公司的势头，并进入了目前每年超过 6500 亿美元的大型科技资本支出周期。 谷歌尚未预订的云积压订单从 4900 亿美元增至 5140 亿美元。

一进去，压力就显而​​易见了。 彭博社将本季度视为对支出是否有回报的考验，Alphabet 并不是唯一面临这一问题的公司，投资者在同一周对特斯拉和七大巨头的其他公司也考虑了同样的问题。

欧盟科技的💜

来自欧盟科技界的最新传言、我们明智的创始人鲍里斯的故事，以及一些值得怀疑的人工智能艺术。 每周都会免费发送到您的收件箱中。 立即注册！

令他们感到不安的是支出，而不是增长。 Alphabet 将全年资本支出指引从之前的 1800 亿美元至 1900 亿美元上调至 2050 亿美元，并表示季度资本支出较上年同期大致翻了一番，达到 449 亿美元。

即使 2050 亿美元也不够。 Alphabet 表示，它将继续扩大租用的第三方容量，作为其自己的数据中心上线的桥梁，这是衡量需求超出其可建设能力的速度的指标。

该法案将自由现金流推至负 59 亿美元，这是近二十年来首次出现季度流出。

尽管收入超出预期，但股价在延长交易中下跌了约 5%，这是本财报季常见节奏的一部分，由于资本支出额度高于预期，干净利落的表现被打破。

规模就是故事。 Alphabet 一年内的资本投资支出将超过其同期净利润，其建设资金主要来自增长缓慢得多的搜索和广告业务。 分析师已经开始询问这种差距到底何时缩小。

积压的订单是多头所寻求的反驳理由。 一份 5140 亿美元的合同收入、尚未确认的收入表明，正在建设的产能已经让买家在等待；悲观的解读是，Alphabet 仍然必须在计价器运行期间提供资金和兑现这一承诺。

双方都同意，答案取决于云在资本支出浪潮达到顶峰之前能够自我维持。

总体利润数据并没有解决这个争论。 净利润为 1121 亿美元，接近一年前的四倍，但其中约 980 亿美元是 Alphabet 所持 SpaceX 股份的未实现账面收益。 把它去掉，下面的数字看起来就普通多了。

核心广告引擎保持不变。 搜索收入增长 17%，达到 633 亿美元，YouTube 广告收入增长 13%，达到 111 亿美元，而 Gemini 应用程序的月活跃用户达到 9.5 亿，Alphabet 的第一方模型 API 每分钟处理约 220 亿个代币。

Sundar Pichai 表示，搜索中的 AI 功能正在推动增量查询，同时仍然每周向网站发送数十亿次点击，并且 AI 模式的月用户数已超过 10 亿。

为了支付建设费用，Alphabet 已经依靠创纪录的 850 亿美元融资和首次发行日元债券。 首席财务官阿纳特·阿什肯纳齐 (Anat Ashkenazi) 告诉投资者，除了从本季度开始的 400 亿美元市场计划之外，没有计划进一步发行股票。

尽管有这么多的噪音，结果却留下了他们本应回答的一个问题。 人工智能支出显然正在带来增长。

当它开始收回成本时，以及在等待答案的过程中，Alphabet 愿意再花多少钱，仍然是一个悬而未决的问题。
